tool.name: generate_smart_wordlist
tool.description:
Generate a target-specific wordlist from already-discovered paths.
Static wordlists (seclists common.txt) bang the same ~4000 paths at every target without context. This tool observes what the scanner has already found and derives variants:
- Singular/plural swaps (/users → /user, /product → /products) - User subresources (/users/me, /users/{id}, /users/export, /users/bulk) - Admin variants (/admin/api/users from /api/users) - Version variants (/v1/users, /v2/users, /api/v3/users from /api/users) - File extension variants (/users.json, /users.xml, /users.bak) - Sibling guesses (seeing /users → try /roles, /permissions, /groups, /sessions) - Parent directories (always explored) - Always-useful: debug/actuator/.well-known/.env/metrics/healthz endpoints
Call this AFTER you've done one pass of crawling / API spec discovery — the more input you pass, the better the output. The wordlist is ordered and capped at max_entries. Feed it to ffuf, feroxbuster, or iterate with curl via terminal_execute for surgical checks.

parameter.name: discovered_paths
parameter.description: List of paths already discovered (from recon, crawling, or API spec). More input = better output. Absolute URLs are accepted and normalized to paths.

parameter.name: base_url
parameter.description: Target base URL. If provided, the output also includes absolute_urls ready for ffuf/feroxbuster.

parameter.name: include_versions
parameter.description: Include version prefix variants (/v1, /v2, /api/v1, etc). Default true.

parameter.name: include_extensions
parameter.description: Include file extension variants (.json, .xml, .bak, .old). Default true.

returns.description: input_paths count, generated_count, wordlist list of paths, absolute_urls list (if base_url provided), usage note.
